homicsx.homogenization

homicsx.homogenization.stress_to_voigt_components(stress, dim)[source]

Return stress components in Voigt-style order.

Parameters:

  • stress – UFL stress tensor.

  • dim – Problem dimension.

Returns:

List of UFL scalar expressions.

Return type:

list

Notes

The ordering is: - 2D: [s11, s22, s12] - 3D: [s11, s22, s33, s12, s13, s23]

homicsx.homogenization.average_stress_vector(mesh_obj, stress, dim)[source]

Compute the volume-averaged stress vector in Voigt notation.

homicsx.homogenization.solve_linear_homogenization(mesh_obj, cell_tags, facet_tags, assignment, settings, physical_tags, domain_size, matrix_phase_id=0, mode='complete', petsc_options=None)[source]

Computes the effective (homogenized) stiffness tensor of an RVE using periodic BCs.

The function applies elementary macro-strains to the RVE, solves for the fluctuation displacement fields, and computes the volume-averaged stress to populate the constitutive matrix (C_hom).

Parameters:

  • mesh_obj (dolfinx.mesh.Mesh) – The computational mesh of the RVE.

  • cell_tags (dolfinx.mesh.MeshTags) – Tags for different material phases (Inclusions/Matrix).

  • assignment (MaterialAssignment) – Constitutive properties for each phase.

  • settings (ProblemSettings) – Simulation parameters (dimension, 2D formulation, etc.).

  • mode (str, default "complete") –

    • “complete”: Solves for all components of the C matrix (3 in 2D, 6 in 3D).

    • “partial”: Assumes macro-isotropy; solves only for unique components to reduce computational cost.

Returns:

Contains the homogenized stiffness matrix (C_hom), average stresses, and the full fluctuation displacement fields for each load case.

Return type:

HomogenizationResult

Notes

  • Fluctuation Field: The solution solved here is only the periodic fluctuation ‘v’. The total displacement is u = E:x + v.

  • Stability: Currently restricted to linear problems due to known instabilities in nonlinear MPC implementations within dolfinx_mpc.

class homicsx.homogenization.AdaptiveSettings(initial_step_ratio=0.025, min_step=1e-06, max_step_ratio=0.2, target_iters_min=4, target_iters_max=8, growth_factor=1.5, cutback_factor=0.5)[source]

Bases: object

Configuration for adaptive time-stepping in nonlinear solvers.

This class defines the parameters used to dynamically adjust the increment size based on the convergence behavior of the Newton-Raphson iterations.

Variables:

  • initial_step_ratio (float, default 0.025) – The fraction of the total simulation time for the first increment.

  • min_step (float, default 1e-6) – The absolute minimum allowed step size before the solver terminates.

  • max_step_ratio (float, default 0.2) – The maximum allowed step size as a fraction of total time. Prevents the solver from taking too large jumps that miss physics.

  • target_iters_min (int, default 4) – Lower bound for Newton iterations. If converged in fewer steps, the next increment will be increased.

  • target_iters_max (int, default 8) – Upper bound for Newton iterations. If convergence takes more steps, the next increment will be reduced.

  • growth_factor (float, default 1.5) – Multiplier used to increase the step size when convergence is fast.

  • cutback_factor (float, default 0.5) – Multiplier used to reduce the step size when convergence is slow or fails (cut-back).

homicsx.homogenization.plot_homogenization_summary(summary, dim, save=False, save_prefix='summary', show=True)[source]

Plot comprehensive nonlinear homogenization curves for 2D and 3D based on the provided summary.

homicsx.homogenization.plot_each_load_case(summary, dim, save=False, save_prefix='load_case', show=True)[source]

Plot comprehensive nonlinear homogenization curves per load case for 2D and 3D based on the provided summary.

homicsx.homogenization.solve_nonlinear_homogenization(mesh_obj, cell_tags, facet_tags, assignment, settings, physical_tags, domain_size, matrix_phase_id=0, tangent_every=1, tangent_delta=1e-06, output_prefix='rve', max_strain=0.2, loading_modes=None, adaptive_settings=None, xdmf_opt=False, csv_opt=False)[source]

Solves the nonlinear homogenization problem for an RVE under large deformations.

This function sets up a variational problem based on the total potential energy Pi. It accounts for multiple material phases with different hyperelastic constitutive laws. The periodic boundary conditions are enforced through a MultiPointConstraint (MPC) compatible with nonlinear solvers.

The solver computes the evolution of the homogenized stress tensor (First Piola-Kirchhoff) and the consistent tangent moduli across various loading paths.

Parameters:

  • mesh_obj (dolfinx.mesh.Mesh) – The computational RVE mesh.

  • assignment (MaterialAssignment) – A mapping where each phase provides its ‘psi_form(F)’ (strain energy density).

  • tangent_every (int, default 1) – Frequency of consistent tangent operator computation (at every N-th step).

  • tangent_delta (float, default 1e-6) – Perturbation size for numerical tangent calculation (if used).

  • max_strain (float, default 0.2) – The final magnitude of applied macro-deformation (e.g., 20% strain).

  • loading_modes (list of str, optional) – Cases of loading to apply. 2D modes must be a selection from [“uniaxial_x”, “uniaxial_y”, “biaxial”, “shear_xy”, “iso_stretch”]. Defaults to all. 3D modes must be a selection from [“uniaxial_x”, “uniaxial_y”, “uniaxial_z”, “biaxial”, “triaxial”, “shear_xy”, “shear_xz”, “shear_yz”, “iso_stretch”]. Defaults to all.

  • adaptive_settings (AdaptiveSettings, optional) – Parameters for the adaptive time-stepping algorithm.

  • xdmf_opt (bool, default False) – If True, saves displacement and stress fields for visualization in ParaView.

  • csv_opt (bool, default False) – Option to save history to CSV.

Returns:

A summary object containing stress-strain histories and tangent moduli for all load cases.

Return type:

dict

Notes

  • Variational Formulation: Uses UFL’s automatic differentiation (ufl.derivative) to compute the Residual (Internal Forces) and Jacobian (Tangent Stiffness).

  • Solver: Defaults to a Newton-Raphson scheme with MUMPS as the linear sub-solver for robustness in high-contrast or complex RVEs.
